subtitle: Daraxonrasib EAP Greenlit by FDA + Pancreatic PARP Maintenance Update + Novel c-Met ADC in mCRC
feed_desc: FDA 'safe to proceed' letter authorizing an Expanded Access Program for daraxonrasib in previously treated metastatic PDAC (Revolution Medicines), long-term update of maintenance rucaparib in BRCA1/2 or PALB2-mutant PDAC (JAMA Oncology), first phase I of the c-Met ADC telisotuzumab adizutecan (ABBV-400) in late-line mCRC (JCO), NEJM Clinical Practice review on Barrett's esophagus, and ASCO 2026 Educational Book on immunotherapy in localized CRC.

papers.title: JAMA Oncology: Long-Term Outcomes of BRCA1/2 or PALB2-Mutant Pancreatic Cancer Treated With Maintenance Rucaparib — Median OS 23.5 Months, Sustained Long Responders Beyond 4 Years
papers.authors: Reiss KA, Xia M, Akamandisa M, et al. (Penn / Abramson Cancer Center)
papers.score_rationale: Base: JAMA Oncology (7) + Phase II maintenance trial (+2) + Biomarker-guided/precision (+1) + Survival benefit signal (+2) = 12
papers.summary: Long-term follow-up of the investigator-initiated phase II trial of maintenance rucaparib in platinum-sensitive advanced pancreatic cancer with germline or somatic BRCA1, BRCA2 or PALB2 pathogenic variants (n=42 evaluable: 27 germline BRCA2, 7 germline BRCA1, 6 germline PALB2, 2 somatic BRCA2). Reiss and colleagues report a 6-month PFS rate of 59.5%, median PFS 13.1 months, and median OS 23.5 months. Among 36 patients with measurable disease, ORR was 41.7% (3 CRs, 12 PRs). Crucially, with extended follow-up 7 of 42 patients remained progression-free for over 4 years, including patients who discontinued rucaparib for AEs years earlier without progression — supporting durable, biology-driven benefit in HR-deficient PDAC and reinforcing the case for genomic profiling at diagnosis.
papers.post_angle: Maintenance PARP inhibition is a real long-term strategy in HR-deficient PDAC. Test germline + somatic BRCA1/2 and PALB2 in every metastatic pancreatic case. #PDAC #PARP #PrecisionOncology #GIOnc

papers.title: FDA Authorizes Expanded Access Program for Daraxonrasib (RAS(ON) Multi-Selective Inhibitor) in Previously Treated Metastatic Pancreatic Cancer
papers.score_rationale: Base: FDA regulatory action (8) + Survival benefit underpinning the EAP (PANOVA-3-adjacent / RASolute 302 OS HR 0.40) (+2) = 10
papers.summary: On May 1, 2026, the FDA issued a 'safe to proceed' letter to Revolution Medicines authorizing initiation of an Expanded Access Program (EAP) for daraxonrasib (RMC-6236), an investigational oral RAS(ON) multi-selective inhibitor, in adults with previously treated metastatic pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma. The EAP is grounded in the prior pivotal phase 3 RASolute 302 readout (median OS 13.2 vs 6.7 months vs SoC chemo, HR 0.40, p<0.0001 — to be presented at the 2026 ASCO Plenary on May 31). Per FDA EAP regulations, requests must be initiated by a licensed treating physician (not patients/caregivers); Revolution Medicines is opening sites as quickly as possible and asks physicians to contact medinfo@revmed.com. This is a meaningful pre-approval pathway for KRAS-mutant 2L+ PDAC patients with no satisfactory alternative — and a bridge until full FDA approval following the Plenary data.
papers.post_angle: If you have a 2L+ KRAS-mutant metastatic PDAC patient who is out of standard options, the daraxonrasib EAP is now real. Physicians initiate via medinfo@revmed.com. #PDAC #KRAS #ExpandedAccess #GIOnc

papers.title: JCO Phase I: Telisotuzumab Adizutecan (Temab-A, ABBV-400) — A Novel c-Met TOP1i Antibody-Drug Conjugate Achieves 15.6% ORR in Late-Line MSS mCRC
papers.authors: Sharma MR, Powderly J, Kuboki Y, Strickler JH, et al.
papers.score_rationale: Base: JCO (8) + Phase I (~+1) + Biomarker-driven (c-Met) novel ADC payload (+1) = 10
papers.summary: First-in-human phase I of telisotuzumab adizutecan (Temab-A / ABBV-400), a c-Met-targeting antibody conjugated to a novel topoisomerase-1 inhibitor payload, in 122 patients with KRAS wild-type, MSS/pMMR metastatic colorectal cancer. The 3.0 mg/kg q3w dose was the MTD; across all doses the overall response rate was 15.6% (95% CI 9.6-23.2), disease control rate 74.6%, median duration of response 5.9 months, median PFS 4.6 months, and median OS 10.4 months — with higher activity at 2.4 and 3.0 mg/kg. AEs were predominantly GI (78%) and hematologic (71%), with treatment-related discontinuations 10% and treatment-related deaths 3%. Selected dose 2.4 mg/kg q3w for further development. A meaningful new biomarker-defined option in heavily pre-treated MSS mCRC.
papers.post_angle: The c-Met ADC space is finally moving in MSS mCRC. 15.6% ORR / 74.6% DCR in late-line is signal worth tracking. Watch for randomized data. #mCRC #ADC #cMET #GIOnc

papers.title: NEJM Clinical Practice: Barrett's Esophagus — Modern Surveillance, Risk Stratification & Endoscopic Eradication Therapy
papers.authors: Fitzgerald RC. (Early Cancer Institute, University of Cambridge)
papers.score_rationale: Base: NEJM (10) — Clinical Practice review; NEJM safety-net rule applies
papers.summary: Definitive NEJM Clinical Practice review by Fitzgerald on Barrett's esophagus as the primary precursor to esophageal adenocarcinoma. The article reframes diagnosis (≥1 cm columnar segment with intestinal metaplasia + goblet cells), articulates a risk-stratified surveillance strategy aimed at catching high-grade dysplasia and intramucosal cancer at a stage curable by endoscopic resection plus ablation (vs morbid esophagectomy or palliative chemo), and underscores the under-diagnosis problem given Barrett's lacks specific symptoms beyond reflux. Importantly for medical oncology referral patterns, she emphasises participation in trials testing detection biomarkers and chemoprevention.
papers.post_angle: If we want to bend the EAC mortality curve, the action is upstream — better Barrett's case-finding, better risk stratification, more endoscopic cures. Required reading for any GI/thoracic onc team. #BarrettsEsophagus #EsophagealCancer #GIOnc

papers.title: ASCO 2026 Educational Book: Immunotherapy in Localized Colorectal Cancer — From dMMR Neoadjuvant Wins to MSS Combinations and ctDNA-Guided De-escalation
papers.authors: Wong EYT, Damle SR, Shiu KK, Cohen SA, Lieu CH
papers.score_rationale: Base: ASCO Educational Book / plenary-adjacent (6) + Practice-changing topic synthesis (+2) = 8
papers.summary: ASCO 2026 educational review synthesizing where immunotherapy fits — and doesn't — in localized colorectal cancer. Neoadjuvant single-agent or doublet PD-1 blockade in dMMR/MSI-H colon and rectal cancer continues to deliver near-universal major pathologic responses and rising rates of pathologic and clinical complete responses; in dMMR rectal cancer, organ preservation via 'watch-and-wait' after immunotherapy is emerging as a credible standard for highly selected patients on rigorous surveillance. The authors weigh open questions (regimen, duration, when ctDNA can replace pathology, MSS-specific strategies including IO + chemo/RT/novel modulators) and call for response-adapted, biomarker-driven trial designs.
papers.post_angle: dMMR localized CRC is becoming a non-operative disease for selected patients. The MSS arm of the field is the next frontier — combinations + ctDNA + smarter trial designs. #CRC #Immunotherapy #ctDNA #ASCO26 #GIOnc

additional.title: Network Meta-Analysis (48 RCTs, 34,106 Patients): EndoAngel Tops Five AI Systems for Adenoma Detection at Colonoscopy
additional.source_line: Eur J Gastroenterol Hepatol — All AI systems beat standard colonoscopy on ADR; EndoAngel led (OR 1.84, SUCRA 0.9), but no system improved detection of advanced or sessile serrated lesions.

additional.title: JCO Editorial: Watch-and-Wait for Rectal Cancer With a Near-Complete Response — Risky Gamble or Safe Strategy?
additional.source_line: JCO — Dossa, Yeung, and Garcia-Aguilar parse how to safely select near-cCR patients for organ preservation after total neoadjuvant therapy.

additional.title: ASCO 2026 Educational Book: Harnessing Artificial Intelligence for the Management of Patients With GI Cancers
additional.source_line: ASCO Educational Book — Goyal, Chung, Mori et al. on context-aware AI across endoscopy, trial design, and EHR-driven trial-matching pipelines for GI oncology.

additional.title: Nature Medicine Perspective: Uncovering Risk Factors in the Exposome for Early-Onset Colorectal Cancer
additional.source_line: Nature Medicine — Lee, Baca and Ng (Dana-Farber) frame the modifiable-exposure roadmap underlying the rise in early-onset CRC.

additional.title: Cardio-Oncology: Carcinoid Heart Disease Independently Predicts Worse OS in Neuroendocrine Tumors (HR 7.09)
additional.source_line: Cardio-Oncology (Emory) — In 270 NET patients, 41% of those with carcinoid syndrome developed CHD; CHD was independently associated with markedly worse survival (HR 7.09).

additional.title: Cost-Effectiveness Analysis: Atezolizumab + Bevacizumab Cost-Effective vs Lenvatinib for Unresectable HCC in Malaysia
additional.source_line: J Med Econ — Atezo+bev added 0.873 QALYs vs lenvatinib at ICER ~RM 51,399/QALY (~0.91× Malaysia GDP/capita), supporting public-payer adoption.

x_thread.label: Post 1 — Hook
x_thread.text:
🧬 GI Oncology Daily Digest — May 2, 2026

FDA greenlights an Expanded Access Program for daraxonrasib in 2L+ metastatic PDAC + maintenance PARP in HR-deficient PDAC delivers long-term responders >4 yr + a c-Met ADC finally moves in MSS mCRC + NEJM puts Barrett's esophagus back on the radar + ASCO 2026 reframes immunotherapy in localized CRC.

A thread 🧵👇

#GIOnc #PDAC #CRC #KRAS #PrecisionMedicine

x_thread.label: Post 2 — #1 Maintenance Rucaparib in HR-deficient PDAC
x_thread.text:
🔥 #1 — Maintenance Rucaparib in BRCA1/2 or PALB2-mutant PDAC (JAMA Oncology — Reiss et al, Penn)

• n = 42 evaluable, platinum-sensitive advanced PDAC
• 6-mo PFS 59.5% • median PFS 13.1 mo • median OS 23.5 mo
• ORR 41.7% (3 CRs, 12 PRs) in measurable disease
• 7/42 patients PFS >4 years — including some off drug for AEs

Long-term data say loud and clear: HR-deficient PDAC is a different disease. Test germline + somatic BRCA1/2 + PALB2 in EVERY metastatic pancreatic patient.

#PDAC #PARP #PrecisionOncology #GIOnc

x_thread.label: Post 3 — #2 FDA EAP for Daraxonrasib in 2L+ PDAC
x_thread.text:
📊 #2 — FDA AUTHORIZES EXPANDED ACCESS for daraxonrasib (RMC-6236), the oral RAS(ON) multi-selective inhibitor, in previously treated metastatic PDAC (Revolution Medicines, May 1, 2026)

• 'Safe to proceed' letter from FDA — EAP can now open
• Backed by RASolute 302 phase 3: median OS 13.2 vs 6.7 mo (HR 0.40, p<0.0001) vs SoC chemo
• Plenary at ASCO May 31, 2026; full approval pathway underway
• Physician-initiated requests only → medinfo@revmed.com

If you have a 2L+ KRAS-mutant PDAC patient out of options — this pre-approval bridge is now real.

#PDAC #KRAS #ExpandedAccess #GIOnc

x_thread.label: Post 4 — #3 c-Met ADC in mCRC (Temab-A / ABBV-400)
x_thread.text:
🎯 #3 — Telisotuzumab Adizutecan (Temab-A, ABBV-400), a c-Met TOP1i ADC, in late-line mCRC (JCO — Sharma et al.)

• Phase I, n = 122 KRAS-WT, MSS/pMMR mCRC
• MTD 3.0 mg/kg q3w • RP2D selected 2.4 mg/kg q3w
• ORR 15.6% • DCR 74.6% • mDOR 5.9 mo
• mPFS 4.6 mo • mOS 10.4 mo
• AEs: GI 78%, heme 71%; tx-related discontinuation 10%, deaths 3%

The c-Met ADC story in MSS mCRC is real. Watch for randomized confirmation.

#mCRC #ADC #cMET #GIOnc

x_thread.label: Post 5 — #4 NEJM Barrett's Esophagus
x_thread.text:
🌍 #4 — NEJM Clinical Practice: Barrett's Esophagus (Fitzgerald, Cambridge)

• Diagnosis: ≥1 cm columnar segment + intestinal metaplasia (goblet cells)
• Risk-stratified surveillance to catch HGD / intramucosal cancer
• Endoscopic resection + ablation curative — avoids esophagectomy & palliative chemo
• Underdiagnosed: most Barrett's patients have no specific symptoms

If we want to move the needle on EAC mortality, the action is upstream. Required reading for GI/thoracic onc teams.

#BarrettsEsophagus #EsophagealCancer #GIOnc

x_thread.label: Post 6 — #5 Immunotherapy in localized CRC
x_thread.text:
🧪 #5 — Immunotherapy in Localized CRC (ASCO 2026 Educational Book — Wong et al.)

• Neoadjuvant PD-1 in dMMR colon/rectal: near-universal major pathologic response, rising pCR/cCR rates
• dMMR rectal: organ-preserving non-operative management is becoming a defensible standard for selected pts
• Open: regimen, duration, ctDNA as surrogate, MSS strategies (IO + chemo/RT/novel modulators)
• Calls for response-adapted, biomarker-driven trial designs

dMMR localized CRC is becoming a non-operative disease. MSS is the next mountain.

#CRC #Immunotherapy #ctDNA #ASCO26 #GIOnc
